name: Munjih ibn Sahm
title: Loyal Servant
shortDescription: The loyal servant of Imam Hasan (AS) and Imam Hussain (AS) who accompanied the Karbala caravan and was bravely martyred.
fullStory:
Munjih ibn Sahm was a loyal Black servant whose mother was named Husniyya. Husniyya was a maidservant of Imam Sajjad (AS) whom the Imam married to Sahm, and Munjih was born from this union.

Munjih grew up in the house of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS) from childhood and served Imam Hasan (AS) and then the Master of Martyrs (AS). When Imam Hussain (AS) departed for Mecca and then Karbala, Munjih accompanied the Imam's caravan with overflowing devotion.

On Ashura, even though he was not obligated to fight as a servant, he could not remain silent in the face of Imam Hussain's (AS) oppression. He bravely entered the battlefield and fought the enemies with his sword.

After a valiant fight, he was attacked by 'Hassan ibn Bakr al-Hanzali' and martyred. The name of this Black devotee, like John, shines alongside the Hashemite martyrs and in Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى مُنْجِحٍ مَوْلَى الحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Rajabiyya:) Peace be upon Munjih, the freedman of Hussain ibn Ali...
